title: Infrastructure and Transportation
description: RAND research addresses the challenges of developing, managing, and protecting energy, transportation, water, communications, and other critical infrastructure throughout the world.
text:
Associations Between Historical Redlining and BMI: Potential Indirect Pathways Through Neighborhood Socioeconomic and Environment Factors and Their Associated Lifestyle Behaviors in CARDIA
We used data from the 1985-86 CARDIA study to test whether retrospective cohort associations between redlining and BMI were mediated by paths from census-derived social and economic neighborhood factors.
Equitable Water Management: A Practical Guide for Utilities
This guidebook was developed in response to growing recognition within the water sector that water services are not equitably distributed.
What to Expect from AI in 2026: Q&A with William Marcellino
RAND's William Marcellino directs the development of AI tools at RAND. He discusses how RAND is using AI, what AI might mean for human productivity, the limits of large language models, and more.
The Weapons of Mass Destruction AI Security Gap
The AI ecosystem may be too narrowly focused on a single threat model: the “lone wolf virus terrorist.” This emphasis on individual actors could leave state-based and terrorist group threats dangerously under-examined.
AI Security Guide and Risk Assessment Tool
This guide is a practical, risk-based resource for those navigating the artificial intelligence (AI) security landscape. The guide addresses security of AI systems broadly, and the accompanying tool provides tailored recommendations for security controls.
Patient Comments About Telehealth and In-Person Care at a Federally Qualified Health Center From Patient Experience Surveys Differ in Length, Valence, and Actionability
While telehealth visits are generally well-received, patient comments about telehealth provide less detail and contain less actionable information compared to comments about in-person visits.
Tipping the Cyber Balance: How AI Benchmarks Could Make Software Safer
Automated reasoning can help protect critical software, build trustworthy infrastructure for AI systems, and lay the foundation for governing advanced AI.
Venezuela After Maduro: Q&A with RAND Experts
Many questions remain one month after the dramatic U.S. operation that captured Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro. How will sudden change reshape Venezuela's future? What are the regional impacts? And how might the U.S.-Venezuela relationship evolve?
Surveying Duality in Space: Volume III, Indo-Pacific Workshop Insights on Developing, Deploying, and Governing Dual-Use Space Systems
These proceedings share details on a workshop examining dual-use space systems (those capable of performing both civilian and military operations) that included space stakeholders from the Indo-Pacific from government, private, and research sectors.
Duality in Space
RAND researchers discuss the challenges of defining and governing dual-use space systems—technologies that can serve both civilian and military purposes—across geographic regions and political contexts.
Surveying Duality in Space: Volume II, Europe-Eurasia Workshop Insights on Developing, Deploying, and Governing Dual-Use Space Systems
These proceedings share details on a workshop about dual-use space systems (those capable of performing both civilian and military operations) with space stakeholders from Europe and the Middle East from government, private, and research sectors.
